I have worked at six technology companies.
I have just moved into AI safety.
I have led teams for over forty years.
Have you ever managed a global team?
Great to see you again, Lucia. Let us do a quick mock interview. So, tell me about your career.
Of course. I have worked in technology for over forty years. I have led teams on three continents, and I have just transitioned into AI safety.
Impressive. Have you ever managed a team through a serious crisis?
Yes, I have. I have navigated several difficult moments, and I have learned something from each one.
And what is the achievement you are most proud of?
I have spearheaded three major transformations, and I have built a track record I can stand behind.
That is exactly the kind of story recruiters want to hear.
I have worked in technology for over forty years.
I have led teams across three continents.
I have just moved into the field of AI safety.
I have reached several milestones I am proud of.
Have you ever worked on something like this before?

People often ask me to summarize my career, and I always smile, because forty years is hard to fit into one minute. I have worked at some of the biggest names in technology. I have led engineering teams, I have spearheaded new products, and I have navigated more crises than I can count. I have reached milestones I never dreamed of when I started. But if you ask me what I am most proud of, it is not a title or a company. It is the people. I have mentored dozens of young engineers, and many of them have become leaders themselves. That, for me, is the real legacy.

Let me give you one piece of advice for your next interview in the United States. When they ask about your experience, do not give them a list of dates. Give them a story. Use the present perfect. Say I have led, I have built, I have delivered. These words tell the recruiter that your experience is alive and still matters today. I have coached many senior leaders, and the ones who get the offer are not always the ones with the most years. They are the ones who have learned to tell their story with confidence. So before your interview, write down five things you have accomplished. Practice them out loud until they feel natural. You have already done the work. Now learn to say it.

I have worked in technology for over forty years. I have led large teams on three continents. I have spearheaded several major transformations. I have just moved into the field of AI safety. It is the most exciting challenge I have ever taken on.
